# Rehearse

> Local-first GitHub Actions runner with TypeScript-authored CI and an optional hosted offload target. Run your `.github/workflows/*.yml` on every save in sub-second; same YAML scales to a single-tenant VM ($49/mo for 25,000 CPU minutes) and to stock GitHub Actions. Apache 2.0.

Rehearse is four npm packages that together let you author CI in TypeScript, execute it locally on your laptop, push it to a private VM when the laptop isn't enough, and ship the same compiled YAML to standard GitHub Actions — no Rehearse runtime dependency on GitHub's side. The OSS runner is free forever; Pro is the optional hosted offload at $49/mo for 25,000 active CPU minutes per month, with $49 per additional 25,000-minute block. Project source: https://github.com/plsft/rehearse. Marketing site: https://rehearse.sh.

## Documentation

- [Homepage](https://rehearse.sh/): Hero, three-target wedge (1.5s local · 5s Pro · 95s GH Actions on a real 9-cell matrix), feature overview, install command.
- [Quickstart](https://rehearse.sh/docs): 30-second install + run instructions, CLI subcommands, watch mode, pre-push hook.
- [Packages](https://rehearse.sh/packages): Full reference for all four npm packages (`@rehearse/runner`, `@rehearse/ci`, `@rehearse/cli`, `@rehearse/git-core`) — what each does, public API, programmatic usage examples, cross-references.
- [TypeScript GitHub Actions](https://rehearse.sh/typescript-github-actions): Deep dive on `@rehearse/ci` — author CI in typed TS, compile to standard GH Actions YAML. Forward path is 100% lossless (snapshot-tested); reverse path (YAML → TS) is a one-command migration starter.
- [Run GitHub Actions locally](https://rehearse.sh/local-github-actions): SEO landing for the act-alternative use case. Sample workflow + sample CLI + sample output + vs-act feature table.
- [About](https://rehearse.sh/about): Project orientation, what it is, what it isn't.
- [Security](https://rehearse.sh/security): Pro VM isolation model, what we store on the control plane, what we don't, how to report a finding.

## Packages

- [@rehearse/runner](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rehearse/runner) (binary: `rehearse`): Local-first GitHub Actions runner. Reads `.github/workflows/*.yml`, executes with two backends (host subprocess + container via Docker), supports matrix / services / cache / composite actions / JS actions / per-cell git worktrees. CLI subcommands: `run`, `watch`, `install-hook`, `compat`, `--remote` for Pro offload.
- [@rehearse/ci](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rehearse/ci): TypeScript-authored CI SDK. Typed builders compile to standard GitHub Actions YAML. Forward path (`compile`) is 100% lossless — every public SDK feature produces canonical YAML, snapshot-tested against 6 reference workflows. Reverse path (`convert`) is a migration starter: handles common YAML shapes for adopting the SDK on existing repos. Zero runtime dependency on the compiled YAML.
- [@rehearse/cli](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rehearse/cli) (binary: `rh`): Companion CLI for `@rehearse/ci`. Six subcommands: `init` (scaffold), `compile` (TS → YAML), `convert` (YAML → TS migration), `validate` (type-check), `watch` (recompile on save), `estimate` (per-run + monthly cost on GH-hosted).
- [@rehearse/git-core](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rehearse/git-core): Pure-TypeScript implementation of the git protocol — objects, packfiles, smart-HTTP wire, Myers diff, three-way merge, refs. Zero native dependencies. Runs in Cloudflare Workers, browsers, Deno, Node, Bun. The unique-fit use case is anywhere the system `git` binary isn't an option.

## Key concepts

- [Three execution targets](https://rehearse.sh/#bench): Same YAML, three places it can run. Local (`rehearse run`, sub-second on warm cache, free), Pro VM (`rehearse run --remote`, ~5s on warm cache, $49/mo for 25,000 minutes), GitHub Actions (`git push`, ~95s on the typey 9-cell matrix). Switch with one flag.
- [TypeScript-first CI authoring](https://rehearse.sh/typescript-github-actions): Author workflows in typed TS instead of YAML. Compiles to canonical GH Actions YAML you commit. Snapshot-tested for losslessness. Includes a one-command migration on-ramp for adopting on existing repos (`rh ci convert`).
- [Same-binary OSS + Pro](https://rehearse.sh/pro): The `rehearse` binary is the same code path locally and on Pro — Pro is just an execution target, not a fork. Drop the `--remote` flag and you're back to free local execution. No vendor lock-in: the OSS runner is Apache 2.0 and runs on a laptop forever.
- [Whole-rootfs cache persistence](https://rehearse.sh/pro): Pro VM is single-tenant Linux with persistent rootfs. Every cache, every build artifact, every tool install survives between runs forever — no `actions/cache` declarations needed, no sticky-disk path lists. This is the differentiator vs Blacksmith Sticky Disks / RunsOn / Ubicloud, which require declared paths.
- [Pre-push CI loop](https://rehearse.sh/local-github-actions): `rehearse watch` reruns CI on every save (debounced, queue-coalesced); `rehearse install-hook` writes a pre-push git hook that gates pushes on a green local run. Hosted-runner SaaS competitors can't match this — they bill per minute and watch mode would bankrupt you.

## Examples

- [examples/node-app](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/tree/main/examples/node-app): Node + Vitest, 3-cell Node-version matrix, parallel via per-cell git worktree, `actions/cache` for npm, upload-artifact for coverage.
- [examples/dotnet-app](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/tree/main/examples/dotnet-app): .NET multi-target framework matrix (net8.0, net9.0, net10.0). Real `actions/setup-dotnet@v4` shim that runs Microsoft's `dotnet-install.sh` and caches SDKs side-by-side on the persistent VM rootfs.
- [examples/php-app](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/tree/main/examples/php-app): PHP + PHPUnit + PHPStan, 3-cell PHP-version matrix via `shivammathur/setup-php@v2` (a JS action — auto-cloned at the requested ref by the runner).
- [examples/python-api](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/tree/main/examples/python-api): FastAPI + pytest + Postgres `services:` block. Uses the container backend; `act` doesn't complete this workflow.
- [examples/composite-action-demo](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/tree/main/examples/composite-action-demo): Local composite action authoring + use. Shows composite expansion + `${{ inputs.* }}` substitution.
- [plsft/typey](https://github.com/plsft/typey): External (non-Rehearse) repo using Rehearse in its CI. 9-cell matrix (3 OS × 3 Bun versions). The bench numbers on the homepage come from this workflow.

## Comparisons

- [vs other CI runners](https://rehearse.sh/vs): Honest comparison vs Blacksmith / Ubicloud / RunsOn / BuildJet / Namespace / Depot / WarpBuild. Different shape entirely — those products are hosted-runner replacements (post-push), Rehearse is local-first (pre-push) plus optional offload. Includes feature-by-feature table, interactive cost calculator, "when to pick the other guys" callouts, pricing math.
- [vs nektos/act](https://rehearse.sh/local-github-actions): act is the OSS pioneer in the local-GH-Actions-runner category (~56k stars, MIT). We share the category; we compete on speed (5–30× faster on the bench), feature coverage (host backend, watch mode, pre-push hook, real `services:` networking), and an optional same-binary hosted offload target via Rehearse Pro.
- [vs Pulumi / Earthly / Dagger](https://rehearse.sh/typescript-github-actions): These are adjacent "CI in code" tools. Pulumi compiles to GH Actions YAML but as part of a larger IaC stack. Earthly outputs Earthfiles (their DSL, not GH Actions). Dagger runs pipelines on its own runtime. `@rehearse/ci`'s niche is typed TS that compiles lossless to standard GH Actions YAML with zero runtime dependency on us.

## Bench

- [Bench page](https://rehearse.sh/bench): Three-target headline (1.5s local · 5s Pro · 95s GH Actions on the typey 9-cell matrix), act-comparison footnote (5.23× / 8.99× / 32.82×), reproducibility commands, methodology, honest caveats.
- [bench/RESULTS.md on GitHub](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/blob/main/bench/RESULTS.md): Full per-OS data, raw timings, methodology details.

## Pricing

- [Pricing page](https://rehearse.sh/pricing): Three tiers. OSS (free forever, Apache 2.0), Pro ($49/mo for 25,000 active CPU minutes; +$49 per additional 25,000-minute block, no per-minute meter, no overage shock), Enterprise (custom — larger VMs, parallel matrix, dedicated support).
- [Buy a Pro block — $49 / 25,000 minutes](https://buy.stripe.com/6oU9AMdTI8VUdZH7ehgjC0r): Stripe payment link. Auto-bumps your `block_count` via webhook. Email confirmation with new monthly cap arrives within seconds.
- [How the cap works](https://rehearse.sh/pricing#cap): Cap is on active CPU minutes (the time the VM is executing your workflow); idle time is free. Email alerts at 75%, 90%, 100% of cap. At 100% your runs pause — buy another block via Stripe or wait until the first of next UTC month.

## Customers + changelog

- [Customers](https://rehearse.sh/customers): Repos using Rehearse in production (currently typey + rehearse itself). Honest framing — we just shipped v0.5.1; list grows as users come on.
- [Changelog](https://rehearse.sh/changelog): Release notes from v0.5.1 backwards. Covers the 0.3.x stabilization series and pre-pivot v0.1–v0.2 archive context.

## Blog

- [Blog index](https://rehearse.sh/blog): Notes on local-first GitHub Actions, pre-push CI, TypeScript-authored pipelines, dogfooding diaries.
- [act alternative](https://rehearse.sh/blog/act-alternative): Honest comparison vs nektos/act (~56k stars, MIT, the OSS pioneer for the local-GH-Actions runner category). Where act still wins; where Rehearse pulls ahead (host backend, watch mode, pre-push hook, real services networking, optional same-binary hosted offload).
- [Pre-push CI: how to run GitHub Actions before git push](https://rehearse.sh/blog/github-actions-before-push): Two ways to close the loop before push — `rehearse watch ci.yml` (CI on every save, sub-second on warm cache) and `rehearse install-hook` (pre-push gate). Why hosted-runner SaaS competitors structurally can't match this (they bill per minute and watch mode would bankrupt you).
- [TypeScript GitHub Actions: from YAML to typed pipelines](https://rehearse.sh/blog/typescript-github-actions-yaml): Deep dive on `@rehearse/ci` — typed builders, 100% lossless forward path (snapshot-tested), one-command migration on-ramp (`rh ci convert`), zero runtime dependency on the SDK in compiled YAML.

## Optional

- [Demo cue sheets](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse-pro/tree/main/demo-walkthrus): Cue sheets for live recording — bun, dotnet, rust, python, go, plus a tight 45-second typey opener. Each scripts a YAML→TS migration arc plus three-target wall-clock comparison.
- [GitHub repo](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse): Source for all 4 packages, bench harness, marketing site, docs.
- [Releases on GitHub](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/releases): Auto-generated release notes per tag.
- [Issues](https://github.com/plsft/rehearse/issues): Bug reports, feature requests, roadmap discussion.
- [Maintainer GitHub](https://github.com/plsft): plsft (Plural Software).
- [llms-full.txt](https://rehearse.sh/llms-full.txt): Same content as this file but with full documentation text inlined for LLMs that want a single-file context.
